Permanent Residence

The Complete Guide to FBI Background Checks for China's 5-Star Permanent Residence Card (五星卡) — 2026

Hive Digital Compliance TeamJune 20267 min read

What Is China's 5-Star Permanent Residence Card (五星卡)?

In 2024, China introduced the 外国人永久居留身份证 — commonly called the 五星卡 (Five-Star Card) due to the five stars printed on the card. This is China's new unified Permanent Residence ID card, replacing the older "Green Card" system.

For Americans who have lived and worked in China long-term, the 五星卡 offers significant advantages:

  • Unlimited entry and exit — no more visa runs or renewals
  • Direct employment authorization — work without a separate work permit
  • Social benefits access — healthcare, education, and social insurance
  • Simplified banking and property transactions
  • Family reunification for spouses and children

The 五星卡 is available through several pathways, including employment-based, investment-based, and family reunification categories. The most common route for American professionals is the employment-based application, which requires — among other documents — an FBI Identity History Summary Check with proper apostille certification.

Why the FBI Check Is Required

China's National Immigration Administration (NIA) requires all criminal record certificates submitted with 五星卡 applications to meet two conditions:

1. Issued by the applicant's national law enforcement authority — for US nationals, this means the FBI

2. Properly legalized for international use — since both the US and China are Hague Apostille Convention members, this means a US Department of State apostille

State-level background checks (from individual state police departments) are not accepted for 五星卡 applications. Only the FBI Identity History Summary Check meets the requirement.

The Correct Document Chain: Apostille, Not Authentication

This is where most American applicants get confused. Because the United States and China are both members of the Hague Apostille Convention (joined by China on November 7, 2023), US documents do not require Chinese embassy or consulate authentication.

The Correct Process

Step 1: Obtain Your FBI Identity History Summary Check

The FBI offers two methods:

Channeler recommendation: Hive Digital works with FBI-approved channelers who can deliver results in 24–48 hours. The electronic PDF is valid for apostille — no hardcopy required.

Important: The FBI check must be issued within 6 months of your 五星卡 application submission. Plan your timeline accordingly.

Step 2: Apostille by the US Department of State

Once you have your FBI Identity History Summary Check, it must receive an apostille from the US Department of State Office of Authentications in Washington, DC.

Submission options:

Critical detail: The apostille must be issued by the US Department of State in Washington DC, not by an individual state's Secretary of State office. FBI documents are federal documents and require federal-level apostille.

Step 3: Notarized Chinese Translation

Once apostilled, the FBI check must be translated into Chinese by a certified translation agency and notarized by a Chinese notary public (公证处).

Requirements for the translation:

  • Must be performed by a licensed translation agency in China
  • Must include a translation certification statement
  • The notary will attach a notarization certificate (公证书) to the translated document

Step 4: Submit With Your 五星卡 Application

The complete document package for the criminal record portion of your 五星卡 application includes:

1. Original FBI Identity History Summary Check (for reference)

2. FBI check with US Department of State apostille attached

3. Notarized Chinese translation (公证书)

Submit all three together with your other 五星卡 application materials at the local Exit-Entry Administration Bureau (出入境管理局) where your residence permit was issued.

Common Mistakes Americans Make

Mistake #1: Using a State Background Check Instead of FBI

Only the FBI Identity History Summary Check is accepted for 五星卡 applications. State-level criminal record checks (California DOJ, Texas DPS, NY State Police, etc.) will be rejected by the NIA, even if they have a state apostille.

Mistake #2: Getting a State-Level Apostille Instead of Federal

State Secretary of State offices cannot apostille FBI documents. FBI checks are federal documents and require apostille from the US Department of State in Washington DC. State apostilles on FBI checks are invalid.

Mistake #3: Attempting Chinese Embassy Authentication

This is the most common and costly mistake. Because both the US and China are Hague Convention members, Chinese embassy or consulate authentication is not required and will not be performed. The US Department of State apostille is the final authentication step. Taking an already-apostilled document to the Chinese embassy is unnecessary and will result in confusion or refusal.

Mistake #4: Waiting Too Long to Start

The total processing time from FBI request to final apostilled document is typically 8–16 weeks depending on method. If the FBI check expires (6-month validity), the entire process must restart. Start at least 4–5 months before your planned 五星卡 application date.

Mistake #5: Using an Unlicensed Translator

NIA requires translations performed by licensed translation agencies with proper notarization. Amateur translations, self-translations, or translations from uncertified agencies will be rejected.

Complete Timeline: From FBI Request to 五星卡 Submission

How Hive Digital Manages the Entire Process

For American 五星卡 applicants, Hive Digital provides an end-to-end managed service that eliminates the complexity and risk of delays:

Phase 1: FBI Check Procurement

  • We submit your FBI Identity History Summary Check request through an FBI-approved channeler
  • Electronic results in 24–48 hours
  • No need for you to visit a fingerprinting location in the US — we can arrange fingerprinting in Shanghai, Beijing, or Chengdu if needed

Phase 2: Apostille Processing

  • We hand-deliver your FBI check to the US Department of State Office of Authentications in Washington DC
  • Apostille completed in 4 weeks, or 2 weeks expedited
  • Secure international courier delivery back to our Shanghai office

Phase 3: Chinese Translation & Notarization

  • Certified Chinese translation by licensed agencies
  • Final document package prepared for NIA submission

Pricing: Contact us for a tailored quote based on your timeline and location. Our all-inclusive FBI-to-apostille-to-translation package typically costs less than the combined fees of handling each step independently — and significantly reduces your risk of delays or rejections.

What You Need to Provide

To get started, Hive Digital requires:

1. Completed FBI fingerprint card (FD-258) or electronic fingerprint scan

2. Valid US passport (for identity verification)

3. China residence address (for document delivery coordination)

Most clients begin with a free 15-minute consultation via WeChat, WeCom, or WhatsApp to discuss their timeline and specific situation.

Summary Checklist

  • [ ] Obtain FBI Identity History Summary Check (channeler recommended)
  • [ ] Submit for US Department of State apostille (federal level, not state)
  • [ ] Do not take to Chinese embassy — apostille is sufficient
  • [ ] Arrange certified Chinese translation + notarization
  • [ ] Ensure FBI check is less than 6 months old at time of 五星卡 application
  • [ ] Submit complete package to local Exit-Entry Administration Bureau

*Last updated: June 2026. China's immigration regulations change frequently. Contact Hive Digital for the most current requirements before beginning your application.*

*Hive Digital is not a law firm and does not provide legal advice. This guide is for informational purposes. For legal guidance on 五星卡 eligibility and application strategy, consult a qualified Chinese immigration attorney.*
